regions.name: Andhra Pradesh & Telangana (Telugu)
regions.key_characteristics: Strong emphasis on Taamboolam (betel set), Kankanam rituals, and Vedic traditions

unique_items.description: Elaborate betel leaf, nut, and lime set; given as gift at every ritual
Tied on wrists of bride/groom, specific ceremony
Preferred over powder; for Haldi ceremony
Rice flour sweet made for celebrations
Rice flour for drawing auspicious Muggu (rangoli) at doorstep
Sweet pongal offered to deity
Soaked raw lentils offered in puja

traditions: Naandi ceremony to seek ancestors' blessings before any major event
Odd numbers (11, 21, 51, 101) for coconut offerings considered auspicious
Bridal Thali (Mangal Sutra) strung with black beads and gold
Ariselu and Ladoo as prasad for most ceremonies

regions.name: Tamil Nadu (Tamil)
regions.key_characteristics: Elaborate rituals with brass vilakku lamps, kanjivaram silk, kolam traditions

unique_items.description: Traditional brass lamp with 5 wicks; essential in every Tamil puja
Specific purity level required for Tamil rituals
Fresh cow milk specifically for abhishekam
White rice flour for drawing Kolam (rangoli) at doorstep daily
Specific arrangement: betel leaf, areca nut, lime
Offered as Naivedyam (food offering) to Lord Ganesha
Specific fresh condiment for feast
Specific green mango leaf arrangement for entrance

traditions: Kolam drawn at house entrance every morning, elaborate for ceremonies
Oonjal ceremony (swing ritual) is unique to Tamil weddings
Parivattam (sacred cloth) wrapped around the deity
Panchanga reading (almanac) strictly followed for timing

unique_items.description: Ritual offering basket with banana, coconut, turmeric, kumkum, betel set
Rice + turmeric mixture showered on couple during wedding
Herbal drink prepared and distributed
Kerala-style traditional lamp, often with multiple wicks
Flower carpet arrangement at entrance
Sweet rice pongal with jaggery, special to Kerala
Silk/velvet cloth for deity

regions.name: North India (Hindi Belt: UP, Bihar, MP, Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana)

unique_items.description: Sacred Ganga water in copper vessel; used in all major rituals
Sacred Tulsi (holy basil) plant; offered water daily
Applied in hair parting of bride; marks married woman
Bangles (chuda), bichiya (toe rings), mangalsutra, sindoor set
5 nectars: milk, curd, honey, ghee, sugar for abhishekam
Pure vegetarian items: dal, rice, ghee for Bhojan offering
Cloth offered to goddess/bride
Offered to deities, distributed as prasad
Cloth screen held between bride and groom during certain ritual
Auspicious verse book read during ceremony
Traditional sweet made and offered for festivals
Curd-based sweet offered as prasad
Specific ritual use with husk for Maharashtra
White conch shell bangles; worn by Bengali brides
Iron bangle worn alongside shankha by Bengali brides
Red coral bangle; part of Bengali bridal set
Bengali puja ritual items; dhuno is burning resin incense
Sweet curd offered and served at all auspicious occasions
Rice paste for drawing auspicious patterns (Bengali kolam)
